hud-fidelity.notes: Both panelists land at 7 with reasoning that converges on mine. Implementation-realist independently surfaces my 'freshest is double-encoded (gutter ● + Tier-1 bold)' point and my '7/min restates the shape' point — strong cross-validation that these are real data-ink offences, not idiosyncratic to my lens. Terminal-ux-pragmatist independently surfaces my verb-glyph small-multiples concern (column-1 dictionary unfinished, eye cannot read shape). No panelist argues for a higher score; no new offence raised that I missed. Hold.

four-questions.notes: Pragmatist agrees at 8. Realist drops to 7 on three grounds: (a) sparkline conflates stuck/idle, (b) exception × truncation collides at row-end, (c) latest is over-encoded. Point (a) is in my own rationale already; I noted it without docking hard because the criterion is whether all four are answerable by glance, not whether each glance is unambiguous. Point (b) is a feasibility concern, not a four-questions one — it lives correctly under terminal-feasibility. Point (c) is real but is the SAME offence as my hud-fidelity 'double-encoded freshest' — counting it again under four-questions would double-penalise one ink decision. Challenge: 8 still reads correctly. The brief asks whether the four watcher questions are answered, and this design attempts all four with peripherally-perceivable signals (sparkline shape, gutter mass, end-row badge, gutter dot) — that is the canonical 8. A 9 would require the stuck/idle and over-encoded-latest issues resolved; a 7 would require one of the four to fail outright by glance, which none does.

terminal-feasibility.notes: Both panelists at 5. Three of their points are lens-relevant, not just implementation-realist: (1) Codepoint widths — ● (U+25CF), ✓/✗, and ⚠ are EastAsianWidth=Ambiguous/Wide; if column 0 sometimes renders 2 cells, the gutter no longer aligns down the band and the small-multiples instrument breaks visually. That is an information-design failure, not a build failure. (2) No verb tag on log entries (`{ message, ts, source?, tone? }`) means the renderer would regex-classify message strings to populate column 1 — i.e. small-multiples discipline cannot be enforced from the data, only guessed at render-time. This directly threatens the column-1-as-shape read my ASI depends on. (3) Sparkline contract entirely deferred to Open Q1/Q2/Q7 — I called the design 'implementable' but the wiring (window, buckets, refresh, scaling, empty-state alphabet) is unspecified, which means the sparkline-test-passing screenshot is a stipulation, not a spec. Concede 7→6. Not 5 because the glyph alphabet IS attainable with width-stipulation, 30-col width IS workable, and my lens weights perceived-shape over wiring-completeness; but the alignment-risk and the regex-at-render verb classification are real small-multiples threats that I underweighted.

hud-fidelity.rationale: Sparkline test passes: the proposed-direction sketch shows '▁▂▂▃▅▇▇▆  7/min' — eight cells, fixed alphabet, inside an existing line of the band. Spellcheck test passes: gutter '●' and end-of-row '✗' both ride the line of work, not a popup. But four data-ink offences keep this off 9. (1) Double-encoding of 'freshest': the sketch carries Tier-1 bold on the description AND a gutter '●' in column 0 — same signal twice, the canonical 'is this ink earning its keep?' fail. The gutter is the new instrument; the bold is reflex and should retire. (2) The sparkline label '7/min' restates what the shape already shows — unless 60s-rate precision is load-bearing, the digits are redundant ink. (3) Per-row primitive count is borderline four (gutter + verb glyph + weight run + end-of-row badge) — at the brief's ceiling. (4) The verb-glyph column is the design's small-multiples instrument, but Q4 leaves dictionary, fallback, and suppression rules unresolved; if column 1 sometimes carries a glyph and sometimes doesn't, the eye cannot read verb-shape directly and the column degrades into decorative chrome. The eyebrow source row also spends a whole row on a 7-char string — low data-ink ratio versus the existing inline-prefix.

four-questions.rationale: All four are answerable by glance in the proposed direction: liveness from the sparkline's existence, pattern from its shape, exception from the row-end '✗' (plus tone), latest from the column-0 '●'. Information shape is mostly perceived directly (sparkline → rate). Two weaknesses keep this off 9. Exception is encoded as a single end-of-row glyph rather than as gutter mass — a column of '✗' marks accumulating down the band would let the eye count exceptions without reading them; the canonical Tufte 'gutter mass → exception count' move is left on the table while the design tucks the badge into the row-end where truncation collides with it (Q8 still open). Pattern via sparkline conflates 'stuck' (recent-but-unchanging) with 'idle' (no events) — both render as flat '▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁'; the candidate flags this implicitly in Q7 but doesn't distinguish a stalled agent from an empty band. Latest is also over-encoded (gutter + bold) rather than carried by one disciplined primitive — but this is the same ink decision penalised under hud-fidelity, not double-counted here.

terminal-feasibility.rationale: All glyphs are attainable in principle: '▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█' is unconditional unicode; '●', '✓', '✗' are basic; the verb glyphs Q9 lists are nerd-font codepoints inside the brief's ≤6 budget. 30-col width is comfortable: sparkline header is 8+1+6=15 cols; the row pattern '● _ description … ✗' fits with margin. Three lens-relevant feasibility losses, however. (1) The column-0 marker '●' (U+25CF) is EastAsianWidth=Ambiguous — in some terminal/locale combinations it renders 2 cells, which would unalign the gutter down the band and break the small-multiples read the instrument is supposed to deliver. The brief should pin a confirmed-1-cell gutter primitive (e.g. '•' U+2022, or a nerd-font codepoint sized in advance). (2) Log entries are `{ message, ts, source?, tone? }` — there is no verb tag. To populate column 1 deterministically, either the producer adds a verb tag or the renderer regex-classifies message strings; the spec picks neither, which means the small-multiples instrument the design depends on cannot actually be enforced from the data. (3) The sparkline data contract (window, buckets, refresh, y-axis scaling, empty-state alphabet, unfocus slide) is entirely deferred to Open Q1/Q2/Q7/Q10. With four states (single-source, multi-source, error-heavy, empty) only one is mocked, and the small-multiples discipline the implementor would be asked to enforce row-by-row is not yet specified.

asi_candidate.summary: Close the verb-glyph column's small-multiples discipline AND make it sourceable from the data: column 1 is a guaranteed single-glyph slot on every data row, populated from a closed three-class dictionary, with a deterministic source for the verb classification.
asi_candidate.actionable_change: In §Proposed direction, replace Open Question 4 (verb glyph dictionary size) and Open Question 9 (glyph palette additions) with a resolved §Verb-glyph column subsection that pins five facts: (a) Column 1 of every data row carries exactly one glyph — empty cells are forbidden. (b) The closed three-class dictionary is: tool-verb glyphs (read=nf-md-eye, list=nf-md-format-list-bulleted, search=nf-md-magnify, edit=nf-md-pencil, run=nf-md-play-circle — promoted out of Q9 candidates into the spec proper), severity glyphs from the existing vocab.ts severity set when the row is a status transition, and a single explicit generic fallback ('nf-md-chevron-right') for any tool call outside the closed verb set OR any unclassifiable message. (c) Stacking verb+severity is forbidden — status transitions get severity glyphs, tool calls get verb glyphs, fallback fills the rest. (d) Classification source: the producer SHOULD add a `verb?: 'read'|'list'|'search'|'edit'|'run'|'status'|'other'` tag to log entries; if it does not, the renderer MUST classify deterministically (e.g. prefix-match on message) AND fall back to the generic glyph rather than emit empty — small-multiples discipline trumps regex confidence. (e) Add a one-line example showing a stretch of rows where the column reads as a small-multiples shape ('              ▶  ' = four reads, one search, one fallback). State the rule explicitly at the top of the subsection: 'one glyph, one cell, every row — so the column reads as verb-shape over time.'
asi_candidate.why_this_lens: The candidate's strongest HUD-primitive bet (after the sparkline) is the column-1 instrument, but the spec leaves it conditionally applied AND data-side unsourced. The brief's lens explicitly rewards 'designs where information shape is perceived directly, not inferred from text' and penalises broken small-multiples discipline. Two facts the other panelists surfaced sharpen the case: (1) implementation-realist's observation that logs[] entries have no verb tag means the column cannot be reliably populated without a producer-side or renderer-side classification rule; (2) terminal-ux-pragmatist's observation that non-tool events have no natural glyph confirms the dictionary must be closed with an explicit fallback, not left open. Until column 1 is guaranteed-populated, closed-dictionary, AND sourceable, the eye cannot read it as shape; resolving this in iteration-1 converts the column from decorative chrome into the second perceptible-by-glance instrument after the sparkline.
asi_candidate.scope: Single-section edit to docs/simmer/activity-zone/iteration-1-candidate.md: rewrite Q4 + Q9 into a resolved §Verb-glyph column subsection inside §Proposed direction; remove those items from §Open design questions; add one line to §Source data noting the optional `verb?` tag and the renderer-side fallback rule.

deliberation_summary: Held hud-fidelity (7) and four-questions (8) — both panelists' divergences either confirm my points or attempt to double-count one ink decision across criteria. Conceded terminal-feasibility 7→6 because two of their feasibility points (●/✓/✗ codepoint width risk for column-0 alignment; absent verb tag on logs[] entries forcing render-time regex classification) are not pure implementation concerns — they are direct threats to the small-multiples discipline my lens scores on. Refined ASI to add an explicit data-source rule (producer SHOULD tag verb; renderer MUST fall back rather than emit empty) so the column-1 small-multiples instrument is enforceable, not aspirational.
